Description: Role-based login and protected route navigation for customers and admins.
Policy lookup and real-time coverage verification.
End-to-end claim submission with full server-side validation.
Customer-facing claim status tracking and history timeline.
Admin approval and rejection workflows with audit persistence.
Admin audit trail view for claim status history.
As a user, I want to log in by selecting my role (CUSTOMER or ADMIN) so that I can access the appropriate section of the application.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. Login page displays a role selector with CUSTOMER and ADMIN options.
2. Selecting CUSTOMER and clicking Login navigates to the customer dashboard.
3. Selecting ADMIN and clicking Login navigates to the admin dashboard.
4. Auth state is persisted in local storage using Zustand.
5. Clicking Logout clears auth state and redirects to login page.

Description: As an unauthenticated user, I want to be redirected to the login page when I try to access a protected route so that the system is secure.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. Navigating to /dashboard while unauthenticated redirects to /login.
2. Navigating to /admin while unauthenticated redirects to /login.
3. Navigating to /admin as a CUSTOMER role redirects to an Unauthorised page.
4. After login, the user is returned to their intended destination.

Description: As a customer, I want to look up my insurance policy by policy number so that I can view my coverage details before submitting a claim.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. A policy number input field is displayed on the PolicyLookupPage.
2. Entering a valid policy number and submitting shows the policy details.
3. The response includes policy number, customer ID, status, effective date, expiry date, and coverage limit.
4. Entering a non-existent policy number shows a 404 error message.
5. Entering an invalid format (not matching POL-XX000) shows a validation error before the API call fires.
6. The ACTIVE status is clearly highlighted in the policy card.

Description: As a customer, I want to see my policy coverage details displayed in real time so that I know which claim types are available to me.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. Coverage types are displayed in a grid/list below the policy card.
2. Each coverage shows the claim type, coverage limit amount, and active/inactive status.
3. Inactive coverages are visually distinct (greyed out or marked inactive).
4. Coverage details are fetched in the same API call as the policy.
5. An empty state is shown if the policy has no coverages.
6. Inactive coverages are not selectable when submitting a claim.

Description: As a customer, I want to submit an insurance claim against my active policy so that I can request reimbursement for a covered loss.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. Claim submission form collects: policy number, claim type, amount, incident date, and description.
2. Submitting a valid claim returns 201 Created with the new claim ID and SUBMITTED status.
3. Submitting with a non-existent policy number returns 404 with message policy not found.
4. Submitting against a non-ACTIVE policy returns 422 with message policy not active.
5. Submitting a claim type not covered by the policy returns 422 with message coverage not found.
6. Submitting against an inactive coverage returns 422 with message coverage not active.
7. Submitting an amount exceeding the coverage limit returns 422 with message exceeds coverage limit.
8. Submitting with amount <= 0 returns 400 Bad Request.
9. Submitting with description shorter than 10 characters returns 400 Bad Request.

Description: As a system, I want to prevent duplicate claim submissions for the same policy and claim type within 24 hours so that the database is not polluted.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. Submitting a claim for the same policy and type within 24 hours returns 409 Conflict.
2. Submitting the same policy and type exactly 24 hours + 1 minute after the first submission returns 201.
3. Submitting a different claim type for the same policy within 24 hours returns 201.
4. The 409 error message clearly states why the submission was rejected.

Description: As a customer, I want to view the current status of my submitted claim so that I know where it is in the review process.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. A ClaimStatusPage is accessible from the claims list.
2. The page displays current status as a colour-coded badge.
3. SUBMITTED is shown in blue, IN_REVIEW in yellow, APPROVED in green, REJECTED in red.
4. The claim amount, type, and incident date are visible on the status page.

Description: As a customer, I want to view the full history of status changes for my claim so that I can see how my claim progressed.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. A ClaimHistoryPage shows all history entries for a given claim.
2. Entries are displayed in chronological order oldest-to-newest.
3. Each entry shows the status, timestamp, and reviewer notes (if present).
4. The timeline visually connects entries to show progression.
5. History entries with no reviewer notes do not show an empty notes section.

Description: As a customer, I want to view all claims I have filed against a specific policy so that I have a full picture of my claim activity.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. A claims list is accessible from the PolicyDetailPage.
2. The list shows claim ID, type, amount, status badge, and submission date.
3. Claims can be sorted by submission date (newest first by default).
4. Clicking a claim row navigates to the ClaimStatusPage.

Description: As an admin, I want to view the full details of any claim submitted by customers so that I can make an informed review decision.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. AdminClaimsPage shows all claims in the system regardless of which policy they belong to.
2. Each row includes claim ID, policy number, type, amount, status, and submission date.
3. Clicking a claim row opens the ClaimDetailPage for admin.
4. Admin claim detail shows customer ID, policy number, all claim fields, and current status.

Description: As an admin, I want to approve a submitted claim so that the customer receives their reimbursement.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. Approve button is visible on the claim review modal for SUBMITTED and IN_REVIEW claims.
2. Clicking Approve transitions the claim status to APPROVED.
3. Approving a claim creates a new ClaimHistory entry with the APPROVED status and reviewer notes.
4. Reviewer notes are optional on approval.
5. The claim card updates to show APPROVED status after the action.
6. Attempting to approve an already APPROVED claim returns 409.

Description: As an admin, I want to reject a claim with a reason so that the customer is informed of the decision.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. Reject button is visible on the claim review modal for SUBMITTED and IN_REVIEW claims.
2. Reviewer notes are mandatory when rejecting a claim.
3. Clicking Reject with no notes shows a validation error on the notes field.
4. Clicking Reject with notes transitions the claim to REJECTED.
5. A ClaimHistory entry is created with REJECTED status and the provided reviewer notes.
6. Attempting to reject an already REJECTED claim returns 409.
7. Attempting to reject an APPROVED claim returns 409.

Description: As an admin, I want to see the full audit trail for any claim including all status transitions and reviewer notes so that I have a complete history of all actions taken.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. Admin claim detail page shows full claim history timeline.
2. Each timeline entry shows status, timestamp, and reviewer notes.
3. The timeline is sorted chronologically.
4. The initial SUBMITTED entry is always visible as the first entry.
